exercises.title: ASCENDING SWEEP
exercises.brief: Order the signal low to high.
promptLines: Define **sortAsc(nums)** returning a NEW array sorted numerically ascending. Don't mutate the input.
starterLines: // TODO: copy, then sort with a numeric comparator
solutionLines: return nums.slice().sort(function (a, b) { return a - b; });
exercises.lore: Default sort is alphabetical — give it a comparator and the stream lines up.

exercises.title: MIDPOINT LOCK
exercises.brief: Find the value dead-center in the data.
promptLines: Define **median(nums)** returning the middle value of the sorted numbers. If the length is even, return the **average of the two middle values**. An empty array returns **0**. Don't mutate the input.
starterLines: // TODO: sort a copy numerically, then pick the middle
solutionLines: var s = nums.slice().sort(function (a, b) { return a - b; });
exercises.hint: Sort a copy, take Math.floor(len/2); for even length average s[mid-1] and s[mid].
exercises.lore: Half the readings sit above, half below. The midpoint is the truth.

exercises.title: SHORTEST FIRST
exercises.brief: Queue the strings by size, stable on ties.
promptLines: Define **sortByLength(words)** returning a NEW array sorted ascending by **string length**. Strings of equal length keep their original input order (stable). Don't mutate the input.
starterLines: // TODO: copy, then sort by length with a numeric comparator
solutionLines: return words.slice().sort(function (a, b) { return a.length - b.length; });
exercises.hint: words.slice().sort((a,b) => a.length - b.length) — V8's sort is stable for equal keys.
exercises.lore: Shortest packets clear the gate first; equal sizes hold their slot in line.

exercises.title: RUNNER-UP
promptLines: Define **secondLargest(nums)** returning the **2nd largest distinct** value. Repeated values count once. If there are fewer than 2 distinct values, return **null**. Don't mutate the input.
starterLines: // TODO: drop duplicates, sort descending, take index 1
exercises.hint: Collect distinct values into a Set-backed array, sort descending, return index 1 (or null if fewer than 2).
exercises.lore: The throne's taken — but who's next in line? Strip the clones and read second place.

exercises.title: WEAKEST LINKS
exercises.brief: Pull the n smallest readings, low to high.
promptLines: Define **bottomN(nums, n)** returning a NEW array of the **n smallest** values in **ascending** order. If n is 0 or less, return `[]`. If n exceeds the length, return all values sorted. Don't mutate the input.
starterLines: // TODO: sort a copy ascending, then take the first n
solutionLines: return nums.slice().sort(function (a, b) { return a - b; }).slice(0, n);
exercises.hint: nums.slice().sort((a,b) => a - b).slice(0, n) — guard n <= 0 first.
exercises.lore: Triage runs bottom-up: the faintest signals get flagged before they flatline.
